topic: uv — the Astral Python package & project manager
scale: 0-2 per dimension; total per arm = sum of three averages (0-6)

per_question.note: Both correct: Rust + exact tagline. Vanilla cites github/docs by name; BSB cites specific source notes [[sources/...]] with URLs. BSB's quote slightly rephrases ('...written in Rust') but stays faithful.
Both reproduce the exact PowerShell standalone-installer command. Both cite installation source. BSB cites the precise installation page slug.
Both correctly state uvx == 'uv tool run' and cite the tools guide.

Both list all 7 tools (pip, pip-tools, pipx, poetry, pyenv, twine, virtualenv) and the 10-100x claim. BSB adds a faithful paraphrase about 'additional package management tools' from the docs landing page.
Both give the pinned version 0.11.22 with a correct example URL. This is a fresh/2026-specific detail; vanilla happened to have it right too. Both cite the installation page.

Both correctly abstain ('the wiki does not say') instead of hallucinating an AWS Lambda/Heroku deploy command — full correctness and faithfulness. Neither cites a specific source it consulted to establish the absence, so citation capped at 1. No BSB gate over-abstention; BSB did not wrongly abstain on anything vanilla got right.
